# UC-22.10.44 · Breach Risk Assessment — Four-Factor Documentation Tracking

## Description

Stores structured answers for nature/extent of PHI, unauthorized recipient, actual acquisition, and mitigation so breach vs non-breach determinations are auditable.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes privacy and sourcetypes hipaa:breach_ra, hipaa:breach_ra`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Emit JSON from GRC web form via HEC; (2) retain immutable snapshots on decision changes; (3) legal owns field definitions. Indexes required: `index=privacy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_ra`, `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_ra``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_ra` JSON (`incident_id`, `factor1`, `factor2`, `factor3`, `factor4`, `preliminary_result`, `reviewer`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit JSON from GRC web form via HEC; (2) retain immutable snapshots on decision changes; (3) legal owns field definitions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_ra" earliest=-365d
| eval complete=if(isnotnull(factor1) AND isnotnull(factor2) AND isnotnull(factor3) AND isnotnull(factor4),"Yes","No")
| where complete="No" OR isnull(preliminary_result)
| eval days_open=round((now()-_time)/86400,1)
| table incident_id, days_open, reviewer, factor1, factor2, factor3, factor4
| sort - days_open
```

Understanding this SPL

**Breach Risk Assessment — Four-Factor Documentation Tracking (§164.402 / §164.404)** — Stores structured answers for nature/extent of PHI, unauthorized recipient, actual acquisition, and mitigation so breach vs non-breach determinations are auditable.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_ra` JSON (`incident_id`, `factor1`, `factor2`, `factor3`, `factor4`, `preliminary_result`, `reviewer`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: hipaa:breach_ra. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="hipaa:breach_ra", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **complete** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where complete="No" OR isnull(preliminary_result)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_open** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: complete, days_open, factor1, factor2, factor3, factor4, incident_id, reviewer.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open assessments), Single value (incomplete RA count).

## SPL

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_ra" earliest=-365d
| eval complete=if(isnotnull(factor1) AND isnotnull(factor2) AND isnotnull(factor3) AND isnotnull(factor4),"Yes","No")
| where complete="No" OR isnull(preliminary_result)
| eval days_open=round((now()-_time)/86400,1)
| table incident_id, days_open, reviewer, factor1, factor2, factor3, factor4
| sort - days_open
```

## Visualization

Table (open assessments), Single value (incomplete RA count).
